---
applyTo: "**/*"
---

# AI Interview Tests

Build a web app called "AI Interview Tests".

## Goal
Create an interactive assessment platform for freshly graduated tech students who want to work in AI-related software roles. The app should help them practice responsible, efficient, and practical AI knowledge.

## Audience
- Future full stack developers
- Frontend developers
- Backend developers
- DevOps engineers
- Tech managers
- DevOps managers

## Core Requirements
1. The app must generate an AI interview test with at least 15 questions by default.
2. The user must have an option to request 10 extra questions, so the test can expand to 25 questions total.
3. Questions must be generated by an AI agent.
4. Each question must be either single choice or multiple choice.
5. Every answer option must have a score: correct option(s) get assigned score, wrong option(s) get 0.
6. When the user submits the test, show:
   - the overall score as a percentage out of 100%
   - the total points earned
   - the correct answers
   - the user's wrong answers
7. The app should clearly show which answers were right and wrong after submission.
8. The questions should cover both technical and responsible AI topics, including:
   - working with AI safely
   - efficient AI usage
   - prompt quality
   - model limitations
   - AI ethics
   - data privacy
   - evaluation of AI outputs
   - bias and hallucinations
   - use cases for different roles

## Functional Behavior
- Show one question at a time or a full test page, but keep the UX simple and clean.
- Allow navigation between questions.
- Support selecting one answer for single-choice questions.
- Support selecting multiple answers for multiple-choice questions.
- Prevent submission until all required questions are answered, or clearly indicate unanswered questions.
- Calculate scoring automatically on submit.
- Display a results page with detailed feedback.

## Data Model
- Test
  - title
  - roleType
  - questions[]
- Question
  - id
  - prompt
  - type: "single" | "multiple"
  - options[]
  - explanation
- Option
  - id
  - text
  - isCorrect
  - score

## AI Generation Rules
- Use the AI agent to generate questions dynamically.
- Make the questions role-aware, so the test can adapt for the selected audience.
- Ensure questions are practical and realistic for junior candidates entering industry roles.
- Avoid vague or trick questions.
- Keep the wording clear and professional.
- Include a mix of easy, medium, and harder questions.

## Scoring Rules
- Correct answers award points.
- Incorrect answers award 0 points.
- For multiple-choice questions, score only correct selections and do not award points for incorrect selections.
- Normalize the final result to a percentage out of 100%.
- Show a detailed breakdown after submission.

## UI Requirements
- Modern, responsive design.
- Clean dashboard-style layout.
- Clear indicators for question type, progress, and score.
- Distinct result state for correct vs incorrect answers.
- Use accessible components and readable contrast.

## Suggested Pages / Components
- Home or landing page
- Role selection page
- Test generation page
- Question card component
- Answer options component
- Progress tracker
- Results page
- Score summary component

## Implementation Expectations
- Use clean, modular, maintainable code.
- Include reusable components and well-structured state management.
- Add validation and error handling.
- If needed, create mock AI question generation first, then design the code so it can later connect to a real AI API.
- Provide sample generated questions for at least one role, such as full stack developer or backend developer.

## Deliverables
- Build the full app structure.
- Implement test generation, answering, scoring, and results review.
- Make sure the app can easily support new roles later.
- Include comments only where helpful.
- Prefer readable, production-friendly code.

## Process Requirement
Before coding, briefly outline the architecture and file structure you plan to use.
Then implement the app step by step.
